Skip to contents
dsVert 1.1.0
  • Reference

Seed and prepare for the per-pair Beaver vecmul round

Source: R/coxNewtonDS.R
dsvertCoxNewtonLoadPairDS.Rd

For a single Fisher pair (j, k), copy the column shares into the canonical x_key/y_key slots that k2BeaverVecmulR1DS / R2DS expect. Two variants: which = "X" -> copy cox_n_Xc_fp -> cox_n_Beaver_A_fp copy cox_n_Xcfp -> cox_n_Beaver_B_fp which = "S" -> copy cox_n_Scfp -> cox_n_Beaver_A_fp copy cox_n_Sc_fp -> cox_n_Beaver_B_fp After R1 + R2, the Beaver z-share sits in cox_n_Beaver_Z_fp; the caller then runs dsvertCoxNewtonFisherScalarDS to produce the scalar share for the chosen term.

Usage

dsvertCoxNewtonLoadPairDS(j, k, which = c("X", "S"), session_id = NULL)

Arguments

j, k

1-based column indices (canonical (own | peer) order).

which

"X" for first Fisher term, "S" for second.

session_id

MPC session id.

Value

list(stored = TRUE).

On this page
